action.reasoning: Observation: baseline ConvNeXt-Tiny scored 0.7722 after 2 epochs and pipeline is valid; full script shows standard mixup/label-smoothing and best checkpoint selected by noisy val accuracy. Analysis: model is likely undertrained, and EMA plus stronger prediction-time augmentation can improve clean-test generalization without changing the validated contract. Noisy validation is imperfect, so save EMA based on noisy val but also include last-epoch fallback. Decision: update training with EMA checkpointing and flip TTA in predict.py, then run an 8-epoch robust training pass (well under 20 epochs) and submit for evaluation.
